body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage — Needs Manual Review

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Decision</summary>

- **Candidate labels considered:**
  - `Mgmt` (category) + `Cost Management - UsageDetailsAndExport` (service) — The issue creates export samples (CreateExport, GetExport, ListExports), but also covers the full package CHANGELOG which mentions QueryResult, ForecastResult, and other non-export models.
  - `Mgmt` (category) + `Cost Management - Query` (service) — The CHANGELOG mentions `QueryResult` and `CostManagementDimension`, touching query functionality.
  - `Mgmt` (category) + `Cost Management - Billing` / `Cost Management - Budget` / `Cost Management - RIandShowBack` — Less plausible but still cover parts of the package that may be affected by the TypeSpec migration referenced in the issue.
- **Confidence blockers:** The issue is a documentation gap report for the *entire* `Azure.ResourceManager.CostManagement` package after its TypeSpec migration. There is no single general "Cost Management" service label in the repository; the available labels are all sub-service specific (`Cost Management - Billing`, `Cost Management - Budget`, `Cost Management - Query`, `Cost Management - RIandShowBack`, `Cost Management - UsageDetailsAndExport`). Multiple service labels are plausible and none can be confidently selected as the single best match. The confidence criterion "no ambiguity between multiple services" is not met.
- **Outcome:** Applied `needs-triage` — could not confidently predict labels due to multiple plausible service labels and no general "Cost Management" label existing in the repository.

</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **CODEOWNERS scan:** Owner lookup was not performed — label prediction did not reach confidence threshold.

</details>
